title: Employer ACA Compliance Profile
description: The system shall maintain an ACA compliance profile for each employer group, including ALE determination, employee count, measurement method, measurement/administrative/stability period configuration, and plan year.

title: Applicable Large Employer Determination
description: The system shall support designation of an employer as an Applicable Large Employer (ALE) based on configured employer-level employee count data, not a hard-coded threshold rule. The ALE determination shall be auditable.
note: 5,000 employees triggers ACA employer mandate. The demo stores the ALE determination as a configured fact, not as a derived rule.

title: ACA Measurement Periods Per Employee
description: The system shall maintain ACA measurement periods for applicable employees, capturing measurement period start/end, administrative period, stability period, and the resulting full-time determination.

title: Configurable ACA Measurement Methodology
description: The system shall support at least the Look-Back Measurement Method as a configurable option. The measurement method, period lengths, and applicable employee categories shall be defined in the employer ACA profile, not hard-coded.

title: Track Employee Hours or Service for ACA Determination
description: The system shall track employee scheduled hours or applicable service data used to determine ACA full-time status during the measurement period.

title: Effective-Dated ACA Full-Time Determination
description: The system shall maintain an effective-dated ACA full-time or non-full-time determination for each employee. Status changes shall be versioned and auditable.

title: Monthly Coverage Offer Tracking
description: The system shall track monthly coverage offer status for ACA-applicable employees. The system shall record, for each month, whether affordable minimum-value coverage was offered to the employee.

title: Distinguish Coverage Offered, Elected, and Active
description: The system shall maintain separate status for coverage offered (offer made to employee), coverage elected (employee accepted), and coverage active (coverage in effect). These three states shall not be collapsed.

title: Monthly ACA Records for Reporting Year
description: The system shall maintain a monthly ACA coverage and offer record for each applicable employee across all 12 months of the reporting year. Records shall be retained for terminated employees for reporting purposes.

title: Identify ACA Compliance Exceptions
description: The system shall identify potential ACA compliance exceptions including: full-time employee with no offer, offer not affordable, no minimum value, late offer, missing monthly data, missing SSN, missing address, invalid plan mapping, and termination date mismatch.

demoScenario.aiInsight: ACM-E025 is ACA full-time based on measurement period but no medical offer is recorded for March 2027. Potential ACA compliance exception.

title: Generate 1095-C Reporting Dataset
description: The system shall generate a 1095-C reporting dataset based on employee, monthly coverage, and offer data. The output shall be a structured data record suitable for review and downstream filing, not a hard-coded IRS form. The demo generates mock 1095-C records as a reporting dataset.
note: The demo produces a reporting dataset. Exact 1095-C codes, affordability calculations, filing deadlines, and IRS submission requirements must be validated against current IRS guidance and Acme's tax/legal policies.

title: Validate 1095-C Data Before Output
description: The system shall validate 1095-C data completeness and consistency before generating the reporting output, including employee data validation, monthly coverage completeness, and offer code applicability.

title: ACA Status Change Audit Trail
description: The system shall maintain an immutable audit trail of all ACA status changes and any reporting corrections, including who made the change, when, and what was changed.

title: Retain ACA Records for Terminated Employees
description: The system shall retain historical ACA monthly records for terminated employees through the applicable reporting period. Termination of employment shall not delete or archive ACA monthly data needed for 1095-C reporting.

title: ACA Integration with Eligibility, Enrollment, Carrier, Payroll, and Termination
description: ACA tracking shall be integrated with the employee benefits lifecycle: hire → eligibility → coverage offer → enrollment → monthly tracking → life event → termination → 1095-C. Changes in eligibility, enrollment, or coverage shall update the monthly ACA record.

title: Configurable ACA Reporting Workflow and Status
description: The system shall support a configurable ACA reporting workflow with tracked status: DATA_COLLECTION → VALIDATION → EXCEPTIONS → READY_FOR_REVIEW → APPROVED → GENERATED → DISTRIBUTED → FILED → ARCHIVED.
